paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.organization: University of Toronto / Meta AI Research (co-authored)
raw_extraction.innovation: Introduces **segment-level recurrence** and **relative positional encoding** to address the fixed-length context limitation of vanilla Transformers, enabling long-term dependency modeling without auxiliary losses. This design significantly improves performance over baselines like vanilla Transformers and adaptive input representations
raw_extraction.pretraining_corpus: WikiText-103 (103M tokens), Penn Treebank, One Billion Word, enwik8, text8
raw_extraction.application: Text generation, unsupervised feature learning, long-document processing
raw_extraction.license: Open-source (likely permissive, inferred from academic publication)
raw_extraction.research_problem: Long-term dependency modeling in language models beyond fixed attention windows
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with segment-level recurrence and relative positional encoding
raw_extraction.paper_title: Transformer-XL: Attentive Language Models Beyond a Fixed-Length Context

raw_extraction.organization: University of Toronto / Meta AI Research
raw_extraction.innovation: Same core architecture as Transformer-XL but with fewer parameters (128M), demonstrating the effectiveness of the relative positional encoding and recurrence mechanism across different scales
raw_extraction.pretraining_task: Causal language modeling with segment-level recurrence and relative positional encoding
